rulings.Case Title: THE CAITLIN ROTH SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST, DATED OCTOBER 7, 1997 AS AMENDED AND RESTATED BY SUBSTITUTED JUDGMENT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #12, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues may include (but not limited to): 1) While some disbursements appear to have been made for Caitlin's benefit over the 2-year course of this accounting, it is unclear whether those limited disbursements are enough to meet Caitlin's "special needs" for which this trust was created. 2) The Court to determine whether this trust is being administered in the best interests of Caitlin and whether the trust should be amended or terminated now that Caitlin is not receiving public benefits and appears to be showing financial independence. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 7th Amended General Order which took effect on 10/30/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ANGELA MOSS SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #8, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/2/21 to be heard together with the petition for fees in the conservatorship case (which was filed after this petition) and for a CIU report to be filed. Proof of payment of that assessment fee will need to be paid, if ordered, prior to approval of this accounting. In the meantime, the trustee must address the following issues: 1) The prayer is missing the fee amounts being requested and the reduction of bond. 2) The order dated 4/19/19 authorized the trustee to retain the funds that were on hand at the end of the 7th accounting period, but she purchased additional or new funds without prior authority. 3) The attorney's declaration re retention of original statements does not contain all of the language required by CRC 2.11 M.4. 4) Attorney costs totaling $975.00 and paid on 3/8/19 do not appear to have been ordered and were not itemized. 5) First Republic Securities was paid almost $6,125 for management fees during this accounting period. How does this benefit the beneficiary/conservatee?

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MABEL ROBINSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/15/2020. 1) Paragraph 9 of the petition refers to Exhibit A, but there is no attachment identified as such. 2) Paragraph 9 of the petition alleges that the petitioner spent 53.25 hours serving as conservator of the estate, but one of the unidentified attachments shows different total hours of such services. 3) Paragraph 10 of the petition refers to Exhibit B, but there is no attachment identified as such. 4) Paragraph 10 of the petition requests $5,342.50 for legal services, but one of the unidentified attachments shows a different total amount. 5) Paragraph 11 of the petition does not allege the current bond amount and sufficiency thereof. 6) Please provide a proposed distribution schedule including a specific description of each asset to be distributed to the personal representative of the conservatee's probate estate. 7) Schedule D shows that the conservatee's tangible personal property was disposed of. Did the petitioner comply with CRC 7.1059(b)(17) before doing so? 8) Schedule D also shows that the tangible personal property was disposed of prior to the beginning of this account, but Schedule 1 shows its existence.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MILTON WISE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/22/20. No courtesy copies of papers filed on 11/12/20 were received by the Examiner by 11/13/20. Other papers filed on 11/13/20 were received on 11/17/20. (Estate proceedings are not "confidential." An affidavit pursuant to Probate Code Section 13100 or original bank statements, etc., must simply be filed as they are, with or without a cover sheet simply identifying them but not marked "Confidential." The original affidavits have not yet been reviewed to confirm that original certified copies of the death certificates were attached to them.) It does not appear that a declaration by either the attorney or the successor executor re retention of the original bank statement(s) has been filed, as required by LRSF 14.93 D. and 2.11 M.4. Courtesy copies of the papers filed on 11/12/20 (and any papers filed after 11/13/20) must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 12/14/20.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: NICHOLAS HOVLAND 2015 SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/12/2021. 1) Examiner does not have the required courtesy copy of the petition. File stamped single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court no later than 4pm on 12/28/2020. (LRSF 2.7, 14.6). 2) A hard copy of the proposed order must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court no later than 4pm on 12/28/2020. (LRSF 14.93). [Proposed orders may not be e-filed.] 3) The ending balance of cash in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. [LRSF 14.62]. 4) Please explain why $397,555.77 is in 1 money market account at first republic bank when the FDIC insurance limit is $250,000.00.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JULIA C. TANDY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Approval To Acquire Reverse Mortgage On Property
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: Petitioner requests permission to obtain a reverse mortgage. It is unclear how a reverse mortgage can be obtained for real property that is in a probate estate currently being administered. In addition, no terms on the reverse mortgage have been provided, it is unclear how this transaction would benefit the estate, and an Inventory and Appraisal has not been filed. Remote appearances are highly encouraged. Please review the 7th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 7th amended general order. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FIRST AND LAST NAME.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF LEONISA A. TRIAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Estate Assets Pursuant To Ca Probate Code 850; Compel Accounting From Prestina Trias From February 2019 To Present; For Damages; Disinheritance
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/29/2020. 1) No proposed orders have been received. 2) Item 1 of the notice of hearing (filed on 08/13/2020) does not state the complete title of the petition to which the notice relates. CRC 7.50. 3) The proof of service does not show notice was given to the attorneys for the conservatee and respondents Prestina Trias and Carlos Macias. 4) No proof of service showing respondents Prestina Trias and Carlos Macias were served in accordance with Probate Code section 851(a)(2) is on file. 5) No proof of service showing the family tenants mentioned in paragraph 15 of the petition were served in accordance with section 851(a)(2) is on file. 6) Although the petition seeks an accounting from Prestina Trias, no citation has been issued and served. Probate Code section 2619.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF THOMAS IVELI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/22/20. No proposed order has been submitted. [LRSF 14.6 A.] Original bank statements have not been file. [Probate Code Section 2620(c) and LRSF 14.62.] Notice of the amended petition was mailed before the amended petition was filed; the face of the notice states that the fiduciary "has filed." [LRSF 14.21 A.] The amended I&A filed on 9/10/20 showed assets totaling $2,904,016.67, but the accounting begins with $2,964,496.15. There are several large transfers or withdrawals or checks, the payees of which are reportedly unknown, and quite a few bank charges for insufficient funds. The attorney costs descriptions do not reflect what was filed or sent by courier or Express Delivery for the Court to determine if each such courier fee, Filing Service cost or FedEx payment was appropriate.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PERETZ-CHAMI FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Property
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/29/20 for a disinterested party to be appointed guardian ad litem or for the trustee to provide legal authority for the Court rely on the findings of a guardian ad litem who is also a beneficiary of the trust. (Neither the ex parte petitions to appoint the guardian ad litem nor the guardian ad litem's report mentions that he is a beneficiary of the Survivor's Trust. Furthermore, a mere general summary of the trust provisions does not adequately represent how the minors' interests are protected. The surviving settlor has a general power of appointment over the Survivor's Trust.) The trustee must also provide legal authority for the Court to confirm assets to a trust that were held as community property with right of survivorship or by an individual or individuals "DBA" and provide information regarding what the partnership or operating agreements of the various LPs or LLCs permit in terms of ownership. (The trustee still has not addressed the language in the trust that provides for the executor or personal representative to make elections, etc.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE KANEY FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Property; Instructions Concerning Interpretation Of Instrument; And For Recovery Of Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Objections have been filed, including as to venue and jurisdiction. 2) It is unclear whether objections are being filed on behalf of Hudson Bently and Brody Bently. They have not signed the objection, but are also minors. The caption of the objection states that the attorney represents both of the minors, and Section II of the petition states that minors "have standing to file this opposition" but only Katelyn Kaney is identified as the "Respondent". 3) Petitioner has withdrawn their request for Matt Quaglino to turn over assets to the Trust. Petitioner has not identified those that are now claiming an interest in the property that are now required to be given personal notice under Probate Code 851(a)(2). These appear to be all individuals that are identified in Michael Kaney's instructions to Mr. Quaglino. Remote appearances are highly encouraged. Please review the 7th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 7th amended general order. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FIRST AND LAST NAME.
